`pfd_lower(n) = pfd_upper(n-1) + 1` by definition — the bins are contiguously packed with no gaps. The strict inequality `>` by exactly 1 confirms correct non-overlapping coverage between adjacent bins.
Monotonicity of bin sizes is a fundamental design invariant for the allocator's bin selection logic. This is a desirable property confirmed by the concrete constant values.

Each bin must represent a non-empty range of word sizes. `pfd_lower <= pfd_upper` confirms every bin can serve at least one word-size class — a fundamental allocator correctness property.
